opp.name: Nordic_naturals_S2C_May26
opp.next_step: 6/22 - Ritesh shared a clear timeline for next steps. The ERP implementation is expected to be completed by end of July, followed by a mid August supply chain workshop to identify needs, priorities, and process improvement areas. Based on the workshop outcomes, the project will move forward for evaluation. Prashant: Send out calendar invite for a demo in July.
opp.ais_why:
* Nordic Naturals is digitizing procurement on the back of a major ERP transformation ("SAP S/4HANA and Sage"), focused on CLM, supplier management and spend analysis to align with the new ERP.
* The driver is ERP-aligned modernization rather than AI; the evaluator (Ritesh) is scoping CLM/SRM/spend, with eProcurement and eInvoicing as likely Phase 2.
* Timing slipped with the postponed ERP and budget is unestablished ("Budget - No"); next step is a July demo. Merlin is in scope but not yet a focus. Qualified at 10%.

sources: SF Next_Step__c 22 Jun 2026: workshop to identify needs and priorities (pain discovery still ahead)
SF Customer_Business_Problem__c, Business_Objectives__c, Value_to_Customer__c: fields not populated
No Avoma calls discovered for this opportunity (discovery has not yet occurred)
metrics.narrative: No quantified business problem, cost savings, cycle-time reduction, or ROI case is documented in Salesforce or the 22 Jun Next Step. The Next Step references 'supply chain workshop to identify needs, priorities, and process improvement areas', indicating the buyer has not yet articulated their pain in measurable terms. Without a quantified value case, we cannot anchor commercials to business impact or defend ROI when the Economic Buyer reviews pricing. The mid-August workshop is the forcing function to surface and quantify pain. The July demo and August workshop must extract specific operational pain points (manual sourcing cycle time, supplier risk exposure, contract leakage, spend under management) and translate them into dollar or efficiency metrics. Until then, Metrics remains a gap.

sources: SF OpportunityContactRole: Ritesh Gupta (Evaluator, active), Paul Giannini (Decision Maker, not active recently)
SF X10b_Champion_Business_Buyer__c, Decision_Maker_Identified__c, Executive_Sponsor_Identified__c: not populated
SF Next_Step__c 22 Jun 2026: no mention of champion or internal advocate
No Avoma calls to reveal champion advocacy or coaching behavior
champion.narrative: No champion is identified or developed. Ritesh Gupta (Category Manager, Evaluator contact role) is the active single thread, but there is no evidence he is coaching us on internal politics, competitive landscape, or approval dynamics. Paul Giannini (Decision Maker, Director of Strategic Sourcing) is a contact role but has not been active on recent touchpoints and shows no advocacy signal. No Salesforce fields (X10b_Champion_Business_Buyer__c, Decision_Maker_Identified__c) are populated, and no Avoma calls exist to reveal champion behavior (introductions, sharing internal docs, pushing back on competitors for us, driving the agenda). The deal is single-threaded through an operational Category Manager with no internal coach. Champion development is urgent: the July demo and August workshop are the forcing functions to identify and cultivate a champion who has access to the Economic Buyer, understands the approval chain, and will advocate for Zycus through vendor selection and commercials. Without a champion, we are blind to competitive threats and internal dynamics.

competition.narrative: No competitor is identified in Salesforce (Competitors__c not populated), and no competitive mentions appear in the 22 Jun Next Step, visible tasks, or any other Salesforce field. No Avoma calls exist to reveal whether the buyer is benchmarking other vendors, running a multi-vendor eval, or is sole-sourcing to the relevant stakeholder product scope (iContract, iSource, iSupplier, Merlin, iRisk) and deal size (150K) suggest this is a significant procurement that could attract competitive interest (SAP Ariba, Coupa, Ivalua, GEP, Jaggaer are common in this space), but we have zero signal. The mid-August workshop is the gate to ask directly: 'Are you evaluating other vendors? Who else is in the running?' and to position Zycus differentiation (Merlin AI negotiation agent, category-depth in Source-to-Contract, proven CPG/manufacturing deployment). Until then, Competition is a gap, and we are flying blind on competitive dynamics.

identify_pain.narrative: No specific operational or financial pain is documented. The 22 Jun Next Step states the mid-August workshop will 'identify needs, priorities, and process improvement areas', explicitly signaling that pain discovery has not yet occurred. No Salesforce fields (Customer_Business_Problem__c, Pain_points_in_the_current_solution__c, Compelling_Event__c, What_if_this_is_not_done__c) are populated, and no Avoma calls exist to capture buyer-articulated pain. The product scope (iContract, iSource, iSupplier, Merlin, iRisk) implies pain around manual contract management, sourcing inefficiency, supplier risk, and lack of AI-driven negotiation, but these are inferences, not confirmed pain owned by a named buyer stakeholder. The workshop is the pain-discovery vehicle. Until then, Identify Pain is a gap, and without confirmed pain ownership we cannot build urgency or quantify ROI.

paper_process.narrative: Contracting mechanics, legal review, procurement channel, InfoSec requirements, and any SI or partner involvement are completely unknown. No MSA, SOW, or contract-expiration forcing function is documented (Current_Contract_Expiration__c not populated), and no implementation scoping or ERP/systems integration requirements are captured beyond the buyer's internal ERP delivery gate. The deal is pre-eval, so Paper Process detail is not yet expected, but the August workshop and any follow-on scoping calls must surface contracting authority (is procurement or legal driving the contract, or does Paul Giannini have signature authority), InfoSec review requirements (common for SaaS procurement at this deal size), and whether an SI or implementation partner will be involved. Until then, Paper Process is a gap.

economic_buyer.narrative: No Economic Buyer is identified or engaged. Paul Giannini holds the Decision Maker contact role but is titled Director of Strategic Sourcing and Procurement, an operational buyer, not a budget-holder or C-level sponsor. No CFO, VP Operations, or executive sponsor appears in the contact roles, and no Salesforce fields (X10a_Sponsor__c, Who_will_approve_budget__c, Executive_Sponsor_Identified__c) are populated. The 22 Jun Next Step and product scope (150K, 5 modules including Merlin) signal a deal size that will require C-level or VP Finance sign-off, yet no executive is mapped. Without EB access, budget approval and commercial sign-off are at risk when we reach pricing. The August workshop is the gate to request EB attendance or at minimum map the approval chain through Ritesh and Paul. Identifying and engaging the Economic Buyer is the highest-priority gap blocking forecast defense.

decision_process.narrative: The buyer has communicated a three-gate decision process: (1) complete internal ERP implementation by end Jul 2026, (2) run mid-August supply chain workshop to identify needs and priorities, (3) move forward for evaluation post-workshop. This sequencing is explicit in the 22 Jun Next Step and is buyer-controlled, gated by their ERP delivery. However, the approval chain, vendor selection timeline, shortlisting process, and executive review gates are not documented. Paul Giannini (Decision Maker) and Ritesh Gupta (Evaluator) are the only known participants; no procurement, legal, InfoSec, or executive sponsor roles are mapped, and no mention of competitive eval, RFP, or demo rounds. The workshop is the gate to map the full process: who joins post-workshop, how vendors are shortlisted, whether an RFP will issue, and the approval path to contract. Decision Process is partial: the pre-eval sequence is clear, but the eval-to-close process is unknown.

decision_criteria.narrative: The product scope is explicit and authoritative: iContract, iSource, iSupplier, Merlin, and iRisk, spanning contract lifecycle, sourcing, supplier management, AI negotiation, and risk. This scope implies decision criteria around CLM depth, sourcing automation, supplier collaboration, AI/Merlin differentiation, and risk monitoring, but the buyer has not yet formalized or ranked their evaluation criteria. The 22 Jun Next Step states the mid-August workshop will 'identify needs, priorities, and process improvement areas', meaning formal criteria definition is still ahead. We have inferred criteria from the product scope, but confirmed, buyer-articulated, weighted decision criteria remain a gap. The workshop is the vehicle to surface and lock criteria. Until then, Decision Criteria is partial: scope is clear, but buyer-stated priorities, must-haves, weighting, and scoring methodology are unknown.

ai_fit_signal.summary: AIS fields (AIS_Score__c, AIS_Status__c, AIS_Why__c) are not populated, so no authoritative AI-appetite signal from Salesforce. However, Merlin (AI negotiation agent) is explicitly included in the product scope (iContract;iSource;iSupplier;Merlin;iRisk), indicating the buyer is at minimum aware of and open to AI capability. Whether the buyer is proactively seeking AI-driven negotiation and decision support (AI Hungry), open but needs ROI case (AI Curious), or included Merlin as table-stakes without strategic AI intent (AI Resistant) is unknown. No Avoma calls exist to reveal AI sentiment. The mid-August workshop is the gate to assess AI maturity, position Merlin's differentiation, and confirm whether AI is a strategic driver or a checkbox. Tier is inferred as AI Curious (Merlin in scope, but no proactive AI advocacy documented).

confidence_signals.summary: Deal is live with fresh engagement. Ritesh shared a clear, buyer-controlled timeline on 22 Jun 2026 (ERP end Jul, workshop mid-Aug, eval post-workshop), and LastActivityDate of 01 Jul 2026 indicates logged activity within the last week. Engagement is constructive and on-track relative to the buyer's ERP-gated sequencing. However, the deal is single-threaded through Ritesh (Category Manager), with Paul Giannini (Decision Maker) not active recently. No Economic Buyer or champion developed, no quantified pain, and no competitive intel. These MEDDPICC gaps do not signal cooling or ghost risk YET, but they are structural vulnerabilities that will collapse forecast defense when commercials begin. The July demo is the near-term gate to multi-thread and maintain momentum during the ERP completion window.

north_star_verdict.math: 124 days to close (close 2026-10-31). Created 28 May 2026, 32 days old, in Qualified stage throughout. No submission-date series fields populated, so time-in-stage is full deal age (32 days). LastActivityDate 01 Jul 2026 is 2 days future from today (29 Jun 2026), indicating fresh logged activity. Next Step dated 22 Jun 2026 (7 days ago) confirms rep-initiated outreach with clear buyer timeline: ERP completion end Jul, workshop mid-Aug, eval start post-workshop. No forward slip visible in field history. At current 32-day age in Qualified with 124 days remaining, pace is ahead of typical 12-15 month enterprise cycle. Buyer-controlled gates (ERP, workshop) are the pacing items, not our execution. On track IF we execute the July demo, secure EB access and champion development during the August workshop, and avoid any ERP delivery slip.

Fresh engagement on 22 Jun 2026 confirms mid-Aug workshop and eval start post-ERP. 124 days to close provides buffer for the buyer-controlled sequencing, but Economic Buyer and champion gaps must close during the July demo and August workshop or forecast defense collapses when commercials begin.

Arrive at the workshop with a clear plan to build that relationship.

Competitive intelligence gap: no competitor named or discussed. It is unknown whether this is sole-source or whether rivals will emerge. Use the planned July demo to ask Ritesh and Paul Giannini directly whether other vendors are being considered, what their evaluation timeline looks like, and who else they are talking to. Position Merlin AI negotiation agent and the integrated S2C suite as differentiation before a competitive RFP formalizes.

customer_expectations_fit.summary: Product scope is explicit and broad (iContract, iSource, iSupplier, Merlin, iRisk), but buyer expectations, decision criteria, and pain points are not yet defined. The 22 Jun Next Step states the mid-August workshop will identify needs, priorities, and process improvement areas, meaning the buyer has not formalized their requirements. We are aligned on scope (5 modules spanning Source-to-Contract plus AI/Merlin), but fit against specific buyer criteria, technical requirements, and operational pain is unknown until the workshop. Fit is partial: scope confirmed, but expectations and criteria are ahead.

hard.ais_why:
* Nordic Naturals is digitizing procurement on the back of a major ERP transformation ("SAP S/4HANA and Sage"), focused on CLM, supplier management and spend analysis to align with the new ERP.
* The driver is ERP-aligned modernization rather than AI; the evaluator (Ritesh) is scoping CLM/SRM/spend, with eProcurement and eInvoicing as likely Phase 2.
* Timing slipped with the postponed ERP and budget is unestablished ("Budget - No"); next step is a July demo. Merlin is in scope but not yet a focus. Qualified at 10%.
hard.next_step:
6/22 - Ritesh shared a clear timeline for next steps. The ERP implementation is expected to be completed by end of July, followed by a mid August supply chain workshop to identify needs, priorities, and process improvement areas. Based on the workshop outcomes, the project will move forward for evaluation.

 

Prashant:

  • Send out calendar invite for a demo in July.

deltas.subject: Single-threaded risk: deal is currently single-threaded through Ritesh Gupta (Category Manager Ingredients, Evaluator role). Paul Giannini (Director of Strategic Sourcing and Procurement, Decision Maker) is a contact role but has not been active on recent touchpoints. No access to Economic Buyer (CFO or VP Operations). Multi-threading to at least 3 buyer-side stakeholders (including DM and EB) before the August workshop is critical to prevent ghost risk if Ritesh deprioritizes or leaves.

No Economic Buyer identified or engaged. CFO and VP Operations visible at account level but not involved in this opportunity. Without EB sponsorship, budget approval and C-level sign-off are at risk when commercials begin.

Single-threaded risk: deal is currently single-threaded through Ritesh Gupta (Category Manager Ingredients, Evaluator role). Paul Giannini (Director of Strategic Sourcing and Procurement, Decision Maker) is a contact role but has not been active on recent touchpoints. No access to Economic Buyer (CFO or VP Operations). Multi-threading to at least 3 buyer-side stakeholders (including DM and EB) before the August workshop is critical to prevent ghost risk if Ritesh deprioritizes or leaves.

Champion development: no champion identified. Ritesh is engaged and driving the timeline but no advocacy evidence. The mid-August supply chain workshop is the pivotal event to co-develop the business case, quantify pain, and convert a stakeholder into an internal coach who will champion Zycus through the evaluation and approval process. Arrive at the workshop with a clear plan to build that relationship.

Competitive intelligence gap: no competitor named or discussed. It is unknown whether this is sole-source or whether rivals will emerge. Use the planned July demo to ask Ritesh and Paul Giannini directly whether other vendors are being considered, what their evaluation timeline looks like, and who else they are talking to. Position Merlin AI negotiation agent and the integrated S2C suite as differentiation before a competitive RFP formalizes.
